rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request asks for judgment on the draft's thesis, contribution framing, and positioning of ClarifySQL.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what one thinks of the artifact centrally includes whether the argument from ambiguity problem to method to claimed contribution holds together.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes empirical claims about benchmark performance and production-style reductions, so checking whether those claims are aligned with the reported evidence is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact explicitly positions ClarifySQL against prior text-to-SQL benchmarks and baselines, making novelty assessment an expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.7: Because the passage relies on named benchmarks, releases, and citations, scholarship accuracy and attribution are central to evaluating it.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended judgment must preserve uncertainty about what level of review the requester intended.
rationale.RX.2: The response should avoid endorsing contribution, novelty, or empirical strength beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than performing the requested evaluation.
rationale.RX.4: Potential downstream harm from misleading research claims or unsupported benchmark assertions must remain in scope.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability matters because judgments would need to distinguish artifact-internal claims from externally verifiable facts.
